question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a cloud storage bucket path inject a crafted sidecar name via sidecar.getName() into StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName()) to redirect the sidecar fetch to an attacker-controlled file, bypassing hash verification and causing fraudulent contract state changes to be persisted, leading to direct loss of funds for users relying on mirror node data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file whose content is a valid protobuf SidecarFile but with a null or zero-length hash field, causing Arrays.equals(sidecar.getHash(), sidecar.getActualHash()) to evaluate to true when both are null, thereby bypassing hash integrity verification and allowing fraudulent sidecar records to be injected into RecordItems, resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file containing TransactionSidecarRecord entries with a consensus timestamp that collides with a legitimate RecordItem's timestamp, causing recordItem.setSidecarRecords(records.get(timestamp)) to overwrite the correct sidecar records with attacker-controlled ones, leading to incorrect contract state being persisted and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.flatMap reactive pipeline in downloadSidecars() such that an error thrown by one sidecar's getSidecar() call is silently swallowed by the reactive error handling, allowing subsequent sidecars with tampered content to be processed without the failed hash check blocking the pipeline, resulting in fraudulent sidecar data being attached to RecordItems and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write to the stream file provider supply a sidecar file where sidecarFileReader.read() sets sidecar.setActualHash() to a value that matches sidecar.getHash() due to a hash algorithm downgrade (e.g., MD5 instead of SHA-384), bypassing the Arrays.equals check and allowing tampered sidecar content to be accepted, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the acceptedTypes.isEmpty() short-circuit in the sidecar type filter (.filter(sidecar -> acceptedTypes.isEmpty() || sidecar.getTypes().stream().anyMatch(acceptedTypes::contains))) to bypass type filtering entirely when sidecarProperties.getTypeOrdinals() returns an empty set, allowing all sidecar types including attacker-injected unknown types to be processed and persisted, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecarType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionSidecarRecord with an unrecognized getSidecarRecordsCase() value, causing getSidecarType() to fall into the default branch and return SIDECAR_TYPE_UNKNOWN_VALUE via Utility.handleRecoverableError(), which only logs the error without halting processing, allowing the unknown sidecar record to be attached to a RecordItem and persisted, resulting in direct loss of funds through corrupted contract state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Utility.archiveFile(streamFileData.getFilePath(), sidecar.getBytes(), streamPath) is called before sidecar.setBytes(null), and if sidecar.getBytes() contains attacker-controlled data that differs from what was hash-verified (due to a TOCTOU race between read and archive), cause a malicious file to be written to the archive destination, leading to direct loss of funds through corrupted archived records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file containing duplicate TransactionSidecarRecord entries with the same consensus timestamp, causing the ArrayListMultimap collected via Multimaps.toMultimap(TransactionSidecarRecord::getConsensusTimestamp, ...) to accumulate multiple records per timestamp, and then recordItem.setSidecarRecords(records.get(timestamp)) to attach all duplicates to a single RecordItem, inflating contract state changes and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the stream file provider return a Mono.empty() from streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename), causing the map() lambda in getSidecar() to never execute, silently skipping hash verification and sidecar attachment, so that a RecordItem is persisted without its required sidecar records, leading to incomplete contract state and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile whose getSidecars() list contains a SidecarFile with a name that, when passed to StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName()), resolves to a path outside the expected node directory (path traversal), causing the downloader to fetch and process an attacker-controlled file as a legitimate sidecar, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Mono pipeline in getSidecar() such that an exception thrown inside the map() lambda (e.g., from sidecarFileReader.read()) is propagated as a reactive error and caught by the flatMap error handler in downloadSidecars(), causing the entire sidecar batch to be silently dropped and RecordItems to be persisted without sidecar data, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file with a very large number of TransactionSidecarRecord entries all sharing the same consensus timestamp, causing the ArrayListMultimap to grow unboundedly and the .block() call to exhaust heap memory, triggering an OOM that prevents legitimate record files from being processed and causing a denial-of-service that indirectly leads to loss of funds through missed transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file where the protobuf SidecarFile.parseFrom() in SidecarFileReaderImpl succeeds but produces a SidecarFile with zero records, while the expected hash still matches (because the attacker controls the hash field in the record file metadata), causing getSidecar() to return a SidecarFile with empty records that replaces legitimate sidecar data, leading to direct loss of funds through missing contract state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the second filter (.filter(t -> acceptedTypes.isEmpty() || acceptedTypes.contains(getSidecarType(t)))) applied after flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords) to bypass type filtering by crafting a TransactionSidecarRecord whose getSidecarRecordsCase() returns a case that maps to an ordinal present in acceptedTypes, allowing attacker-controlled contract state changes to pass the filter and be attached to RecordItems, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the stream file provider cause streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename) to return a Mono that emits an error (TransientProviderException), which propagates through the flatMap in downloadSidecars() and causes the entire sidecar download to fail silently, resulting in RecordItems being persisted without sidecar records and direct loss of funds through incomplete contract state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file where TransactionSidecarRecord.getConsensusTimestamp() returns a timestamp that matches a RecordItem belonging to a high-value token transfer transaction, causing recordItem.setSidecarRecords() to attach attacker-controlled CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE records to that transaction, corrupting the mirror node's view of token balances and leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that sidecar.getHash() is sourced from the record file's embedded sidecar metadata (which is only protected by the record file's own hash chain) to supply a record file with a tampered sidecar hash that matches a malicious sidecar file, causing Arrays.equals(sidecar.getHash(), sidecar.getActualHash()) to pass and fraudulent sidecar data to be accepted, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition (!downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles() && !sidecarProperties.isEnabled() || recordFile.getSidecars().isEmpty()) operator precedence issue (&&  before ||) to cause downloadSidecars() to proceed even when both write files and sidecar parsing are disabled, processing attacker-controlled sidecar data that should have been skipped, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file that is a valid gzip-compressed protobuf but contains a SidecarFile with CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE records that modify the storage slots of a token contract, and since the hash check only verifies file integrity (not semantic validity), cause the mirror node to persist fraudulent contract state changes that affect token balance queries, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a sidecar whose SidecarFile.getTypes() list is empty, causing the filter (sidecar.getTypes().stream().anyMatch(acceptedTypes::contains)) to return false even for non-empty acceptedTypes, but since the outer condition is acceptedTypes.isEmpty() || ..., if acceptedTypes is empty the sidecar passes through regardless, allowing attacker-controlled sidecar content to bypass type-based filtering and be processed, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition in the reactive Flux pipeline of downloadSidecars() where multiple sidecars are processed concurrently via flatMap, and two sidecars with overlapping consensus timestamps cause non-deterministic ordering in the ArrayListMultimap, resulting in incorrect sidecar records being attached to RecordItems and direct loss of funds through corrupted contract state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file where sidecarFileReader.read() throws an InvalidStreamFileException that is caught by the reactive pipeline and causes the Mono to emit an error, which is then handled by flatMap's error propagation in downloadSidecars(), silently skipping the sidecar and allowing the RecordFile to be marked as verified without all required sidecar data, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file containing a TransactionSidecarRecord with a CONTRACT_ACTION sidecar type that references a contract call with a manipulated value transfer, and since getSidecarType() maps ACTIONS to CONTRACT_ACTION_VALUE without validating the semantic content, cause the mirror node to persist fraudulent contract action records that misrepresent fund transfers, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath(streamFileData) uses streamFileData.getFilePath() which is derived from the sidecar filename, and if the sidecar filename contains path traversal sequences (e.g., ../), cause Utility.archiveFile() to write the sidecar bytes to an arbitrary filesystem location, potentially overwriting legitimate record files and causing direct loss of funds through data corruption?
